commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: The PR has a protected maintainer label and needs assigned owner review for the doctor orchestration contract and Linux service-lifetime semantics, not an automated repair PR.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found in the TypeScript doctor/test diff. (sha=5fdf0ff7329130f32c22f8e1cf47688a9071bf1f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces the early all-check doctor repair bridge with positioned structured repair checks for shell completion, startup-channel maintenance, systemd linger, and legacy browser residue, plus focused tests and doctor note-context plumbing.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a structured doctor repair refactor rather than a reported bug. The behavior is reviewable through the PR's current-head code, CI, and posted real CLI proof for the converted repair paths.
commands.review_followup: The PR has a protected `maintainer` label and needs assigned owner review for the doctor orchestration contract and Linux service-lifetime semantics, not an automated repair PR.

target.title: Doctor: convert interactive maintenance checks
target.body:
## Goal

Establish the structured doctor repair contract and apply it to the first interactive-maintenance repairs without changing the user-facing order of `openclaw doctor`.

The core goal is not just moving code. It is making doctor repairs observable and auditable through one contract:

```ts
detect(ctx, scope?) -> HealthFinding[]
repair(ctx, findings) -> HealthRepairResult
```

`detect()` is the read-only source of truth for normal doctor diagnostics, `doctor --lint`, and post-repair validation. `repair()` is the only mutating structured path, receives the exact findings from detect, and reports machine-useful repair output through `changes`, `warnings`, `diffs`, and `effects`.

Human presentation stays on the existing note system. Structured checks can call `ctx.doctor.note(message, title?)` from detect or repair when doctor runs in a human presentation mode. Lint callers do not provide a note sink, so note text is never promoted into lint findings, collected in result payloads, or serialized as a separate Doctor result surface.

## Scope

| Area | Goal | Behavior |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Shell completion | Make completion repair detectable, previewable, and validated. | Detect reports stale/missing cached completion state; repair updates completion state; detect-after proves it is no longer reported. |
| Startup channel maintenance | Keep startup-channel repair at its original contribution point while using the structured runner. | Existing contribution order is preserved; repair result is reported through the shared contract. |
| Systemd linger | Represent guided service/user-manager repair as side effects. | Detect is silent/read-only; repair uses the structured result contract and can rely on doctor presentation notes for operator context. |
| Browser profile residue | Preserve prior repair behavior while validating it through the health-check contract. | Repair is followed by scoped detect-after validation. |
| Doctor presentation notes | Keep notes as presentation, not data model output. | Converted checks call `ctx.doctor.note(...)`; `HealthFinding[]` remains the only lint/CI signal. |

## Contract Table

| Contract piece | Requirement | Why it matters |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Read-only detect | No mutation from structured detect. Human notes may only go through `ctx.doctor.note(...)` when the caller provides it. | Normal doctor, lint, and detect-after can reuse the same source of truth without special note-routing logic. |
| Findings as repair input | Repair receives the findings it is allowed to fix. | Prevents repair from drifting from what doctor detected. |
| Notes are presentation | No `detect().notes`, no `repair().notes`, no detection/repair note arrays in runner results. | Avoids a second note system and keeps `note()` globally suppressible. |
| Lint signal | `doctor --lint` emits findings only. | Notes are never inferred into lint findings or JSON payloads. |
| Dry-run/effects | Preview reports concrete `changes[]`, `diffs[]`, or `effects[]`; no fake diffs. | Reviewers can see what would happen before mutation. |
| Detect-after | Non-dry-run repairs are validated by running detect again on the repaired scope. | A repair is not considered successful just because it emitted an effect. |

## Proof

Current squashed head: `5fdf0ff7329130f32c22f8e1cf47688a9071bf1f`.

| Proof | Result |
| --- | --- |
| `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` | Passed. |
| `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts` | Passed, 7 tests. |
| `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts` | Initial run found one assertion expectation mismatch after the note policy change; assertion was corrected. Subsequent local wrapper runs stalled in Vitest startup/transform before a result. |
| `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts` | Local wrapper stalled in Vitest startup/transform before a result. |
| Dependency repair | `pnpm install` was run once because local Vitest startup initially missed `@rolldown/binding-win32-x64-msvc`; install completed successfully. |

Earlier proof artifacts from the prior squashed head still cover the original structured repair behavior, shell-completion/systemd/browser/startup repair shape, and Azure CLI before/after behavior. The note-policy change in this refresh removes structured note result surfaces and routes presentation through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`.

## Out of Scope

- Exposing new public doctor dry-run/diff flags.
- Converting every doctor repair bucket in one PR.
- Replacing owner-specific repair policy with generic core policy.
- Making note text machine-readable. Findings remain the machine-readable output.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Converted doctor maintenance repairs run through structured health checks at their original doctor contribution positions, while preserving user-facing repair behavior and adding scoped post-repair validation. Structured note handling now uses the existing suppressible note system instead of collected note result arrays.

Real environment tested: Current squashed PR head `5fdf0ff7329130f32c22f8e1cf47688a9071bf1f` was checked with whitespace proof and focused repair-flow Vitest proof. Local broader Vitest wrapper runs were attempted but stalled in startup/transform after dependency repair. Earlier disposable Azure Ubuntu 24.04 proof covered a real user systemd manager and real `loginctl` linger state for the original structured repair behavior.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm install`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts`; attempted `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts`; attempted `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: whitespace check passed; repair-flow focused tests passed; code search shows no `HealthPresentationNote`, `repairNote`, `detectionNotes`, `repairNotes`, or structured note result surfaces remain in Doctor flow/command code.

Observed result after fix: structured checks expose findings/repair outputs only, and doctor presentation notes flow through `ctx.doctor.note(...)` when a human doctor runner provides it. Lint callers can omit the note sink, so note text is not promoted into findings.

What was not tested: The broader local Doctor Vitest files did not complete after the note-policy refresh due local Vitest startup/transform stalls. Fresh GitHub CI should be used for the final broad proof on this squashed head.

## Follow-on Examples

These follow-on PRs apply this contract to the next doctor repair buckets and need to be refreshed on top of this note-policy baseline.

| PR | Bucket | Expected refresh |
| --- | --- | --- |
| #84290 | Plugin registry and configured plugin installs | Rebase onto #83753 and keep findings/changes/warnings/effects/diffs only. Use `ctx.doctor.note(...)` for human presentation if needed. |
| #84326 | Sandbox registry, images, and scope diagnostics | Rebase onto #84290 and route any repair-time operator context through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`. |
| #84340 | Gateway service cleanup and config drift | Rebase onto #84326 and replace any `repairNote`/deferred note result usage with `ctx.doctor.note(...)`. |
| #84366 | Session locks and session transcripts | Rebase onto #84340 and keep file mutation reporting in findings/effects/changes/warnings. |
| #84450 | Config audit and cron-store cleanup | Rebase onto #84366 and keep dry-run output in effects/diffs/changes/warnings. |
| #84472 | Top-level doctor preview surface | Rebase onto #84450 and remove detection/repair note arrays from preview JSON. Preview output should remain findings/effects/diffs/changes/warnings/skipped. |
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

| #84290 | Plugin registry and configured plugin installs | Rebase onto #83753 and keep findings/changes/warnings/effects/diffs only. Use `ctx.doctor.note(...)` for human presentation if needed. |

| #84340 | Gateway service cleanup and config drift | Rebase onto #84326 and replace any `repairNote`/deferred note result usage with `ctx.doctor.note(...)`. |
| #84366 | Session locks and session transcripts | Rebase onto #84340 and keep file mutation reporting in findings/effects/changes/warnings. |
| #84450 | Config audit and cron-store cleanup | Rebase onto #84366 and keep dry-run output in effects/diffs/changes/warnings. |
| #84472 | Top-level doctor preview surface | Rebase onto #84450 and remove detection/repair note arrays from preview JSON. Preview output should remain findings/effects/diffs/changes/warnings/skipped. |

commands.author: giodl73-repo

target.title: Doctor: convert interactive maintenance checks
target.body:
## Goal

Establish the structured doctor repair contract and apply it to the first interactive-maintenance repairs without changing the user-facing order of `openclaw doctor`.

The core goal is not just moving code. It is making doctor repairs observable and auditable through one contract:

```ts
detect(ctx, scope?) -> HealthFinding[]
repair(ctx, findings) -> HealthRepairResult
```

`detect()` is the read-only source of truth for normal doctor diagnostics, `doctor --lint`, and post-repair validation. `repair()` is the only mutating structured path, receives the exact findings from detect, and reports machine-useful repair output through `changes`, `warnings`, `diffs`, and `effects`.

Human presentation stays on the existing note system. Structured checks can call `ctx.doctor.note(message, title?)` from detect or repair when doctor runs in a human presentation mode. Lint callers do not provide a note sink, so note text is never promoted into lint findings, collected in result payloads, or serialized as a separate Doctor result surface.

## Scope

| Area | Goal | Behavior |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Shell completion | Make completion repair detectable, previewable, and validated. | Detect reports stale/missing cached completion state; repair updates completion state; detect-after proves it is no longer reported. |
| Startup channel maintenance | Keep startup-channel repair at its original contribution point while using the structured runner. | Existing contribution order is preserved; repair result is reported through the shared contract. |
| Systemd linger | Represent guided service/user-manager repair as side effects. | Detect is silent/read-only; repair uses the structured result contract and can rely on doctor presentation notes for operator context. |
| Browser profile residue | Preserve prior repair behavior while validating it through the health-check contract. | Repair is followed by scoped detect-after validation. |
| Doctor presentation notes | Keep notes as presentation, not data model output. | Converted checks call `ctx.doctor.note(...)`; `HealthFinding[]` remains the only lint/CI signal. |

## Contract Table

| Contract piece | Requirement | Why it matters |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Read-only detect | No mutation from structured detect. Human notes may only go through `ctx.doctor.note(...)` when the caller provides it. | Normal doctor, lint, and detect-after can reuse the same source of truth without special note-routing logic. |
| Findings as repair input | Repair receives the findings it is allowed to fix. | Prevents repair from drifting from what doctor detected. |
| Notes are presentation | No `detect().notes`, no `repair().notes`, no detection/repair note arrays in runner results. | Avoids a second note system and keeps `note()` globally suppressible. |
| Lint signal | `doctor --lint` emits findings only. | Notes are never inferred into lint findings or JSON payloads. |
| Dry-run/effects | Preview reports concrete `changes[]`, `diffs[]`, or `effects[]`; no fake diffs. | Reviewers can see what would happen before mutation. |
| Detect-after | Non-dry-run repairs are validated by running detect again on the repaired scope. | A repair is not considered successful just because it emitted an effect. |

## Proof

Current squashed head: `5fdf0ff7329130f32c22f8e1cf47688a9071bf1f`.

| Proof | Result |
| --- | --- |
| `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` | Passed. |
| `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts` | Passed, 7 tests. |
| `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts` | Initial run found one assertion expectation mismatch after the note policy change; assertion was corrected. Subsequent local wrapper runs stalled in Vitest startup/transform before a result. |
| `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts` | Local wrapper stalled in Vitest startup/transform before a result. |
| Dependency repair | `pnpm install` was run once because local Vitest startup initially missed `@rolldown/binding-win32-x64-msvc`; install completed successfully. |

Earlier proof artifacts from the prior squashed head still cover the original structured repair behavior, shell-completion/systemd/browser/startup repair shape, and Azure CLI before/after behavior. The note-policy change in this refresh removes structured note result surfaces and routes presentation through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`.

## Out of Scope

- Exposing new public doctor dry-run/diff flags.
- Converting every doctor repair bucket in one PR.
- Replacing owner-specific repair policy with generic core policy.
- Making note text machine-readable. Findings remain the machine-readable output.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Converted doctor maintenance repairs run through structured health checks at their original doctor contribution positions, while preserving user-facing repair behavior and adding scoped post-repair validation. Structured note handling now uses the existing suppressible note system instead of collected note result arrays.

Real environment tested: Current squashed PR head `5fdf0ff7329130f32c22f8e1cf47688a9071bf1f` was checked with whitespace proof and focused repair-flow Vitest proof. Local broader Vitest wrapper runs were attempted but stalled in startup/transform after dependency repair. Earlier disposable Azure Ubuntu 24.04 proof covered a real user systemd manager and real `loginctl` linger state for the original structured repair behavior.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm install`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts`; attempted `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts`; attempted `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: whitespace check passed; repair-flow focused tests passed; code search shows no `HealthPresentationNote`, `repairNote`, `detectionNotes`, `repairNotes`, or structured note result surfaces remain in Doctor flow/command code.

Observed result after fix: structured checks expose findings/repair outputs only, and doctor presentation notes flow through `ctx.doctor.note(...)` when a human doctor runner provides it. Lint callers can omit the note sink, so note text is not promoted into findings.

What was not tested: The broader local Doctor Vitest files did not complete after the note-policy refresh due local Vitest startup/transform stalls. Fresh GitHub CI should be used for the final broad proof on this squashed head.

## Follow-on Examples

These follow-on PRs apply this contract to the next doctor repair buckets and need to be refreshed on top of this note-policy baseline.

| PR | Bucket | Expected refresh |
| --- | --- | --- |
| #84290 | Plugin registry and configured plugin installs | Rebase onto #83753 and keep findings/changes/warnings/effects/diffs only. Use `ctx.doctor.note(...)` for human presentation if needed. |
| #84326 | Sandbox registry, images, and scope diagnostics | Rebase onto #84290 and route any repair-time operator context through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`. |
| #84340 | Gateway service cleanup and config drift | Rebase onto #84326 and replace any `repairNote`/deferred note result usage with `ctx.doctor.note(...)`. |
| #84366 | Session locks and session transcripts | Rebase onto #84340 and keep file mutation reporting in findings/effects/changes/warnings. |
| #84450 | Config audit and cron-store cleanup | Rebase onto #84366 and keep dry-run output in effects/diffs/changes/warnings. |
| #84472 | Top-level doctor preview surface | Rebase onto #84450 and remove detection/repair note arrays from preview JSON. Preview output should remain findings/effects/diffs/changes/warnings/skipped. |

These follow-on PRs apply this contract to the next doctor repair buckets and need to be refreshed on top of this note-policy baseline.

| PR | Bucket | Expected refresh |
| --- | --- | --- |
| #84290 | Plugin registry and configured plugin installs | Rebase onto #83753 and keep findings/changes/warnings/effects/diffs only. Use `ctx.doctor.note(...)` for human presentation if needed. |
| #84326 | Sandbox registry, images, and scope diagnostics | Rebase onto #84290 and route any repair-time operator context through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`. |
| #84340 | Gateway service cleanup and config drift | Rebase onto #84326 and replace any `repairNote`/deferred note result usage with `ctx.doctor.note(...)`. |
| #84366 | Session locks and session transcripts | Rebase onto #84340 and keep file mutation reporting in findings/effects/changes/warnings. |
| #84450 | Config audit and cron-store cleanup | Rebase onto #84366 and keep dry-run output in effects/diffs/changes/warnings. |
| #84472 | Top-level doctor preview surface | Rebase onto #84450 and remove detection/repair note arrays from preview JSON. Preview output should remain findings/effects/diffs/changes/warnings/skipped. |
